title: CASE LOAD Act of 2025
plain_summary: The CASE LOAD Act of 2025 will add five new federal judges to the eastern district of California. Right now, there are only six judges handling too many court cases for too many people. The new judges will help reduce the heavy workload. The bill also provides money to pay for the new judges and build the courtrooms they need.
last_action_text: Referred to the House Committee on the Judiciary.

sections.title: Section 1  Short Title
sections.text: This law can be called the Creating Additional Seats to Ease Legally Overburdened Adjudicators' Dockets Act of two thousand twenty five or the CASE LOAD Act of two thousand twenty five.

sections.title: Section 2  Findings
sections.text: Congress has found the following facts. The Federal district court for the eastern district of California is the Federal trial court that serves thirty four counties. This includes five of the fastest growing cities in California. The court serves about eight million four hundred thousand people. The Federal district court for the eastern district of California is one of the largest court areas in the country. It serves about eight million four hundred thousand residents across more than eighty seven thousand square miles of land. However, there are only six permanent judges. There is one upcoming empty position waiting for someone to be nominated to replace a current district judge who may be promoted. Based on two thousand nineteen census data, there was one authorized district judge for every one million three hundred sixty two thousand five hundred fifty two people. The central district of California has the next highest ratio in the state, which is nearly half the size at one judge for every six hundred ninety four thousand three hundred twenty three people. As of June two thousand twenty two, the total pending cases per judge in the Federal district court for the eastern district of California was one thousand three hundred eight. This is over two and six tenths times larger than the average for all districts within the United States Court of Appeals for the Ninth Circuit. Pending caseloads remain high. If a new vacancy opens when the district judge is promoted, caseloads will once again reach this unsustainable level.

sections.title: Section 3  Additional District Judges for the Eastern District of California
sections.text: Starting on or after January twenty first two thousand twenty seven, the President must appoint two additional district judges for the eastern district of California. The Senate must give their advice and consent for these appointments. The table in section one hundred thirty three part a of title twenty eight of the United States Code will be changed. The items about California will be removed and replaced with the following information. California Northern will have fourteen judges, Eastern will have eight judges, Central will have twenty seven judges, and Southern will have thirteen judges. This change will take effect on January twentieth two thousand twenty seven. Starting on or after January fifth two thousand twenty nine, the President must appoint one additional district judge for the eastern district of California. The Senate must give their advice and consent for this appointment. The table in section one hundred thirty three part a of title twenty eight of the United States Code will be changed. The items about California will be removed and replaced with the following information. California Northern will have fourteen judges, Eastern will have nine judges, Central will have twenty seven judges, and Southern will have thirteen judges. This change will take effect on January fourth two thousand twenty nine. Starting on or after January twenty first two thousand thirty one, the President must appoint two additional district judges for the eastern district of California. The Senate must give their advice and consent for these appointments. The table in section one hundred thirty three part a of title twenty eight of the United States Code will be changed. The items about California will be removed and replaced with the following information. California Northern will have fourteen judges, Eastern will have eleven judges, Central will have twenty seven judges, and Southern will have thirteen judges. This change will take effect on January twentieth two thousand thirty one.

sections.title: Section 4  Authorization of Appropriations
sections.text: Congress authorizes the spending of whatever money is necessary to carry out this law and the changes it makes. This includes money needed to provide proper space and facilities for the new judge positions created by this law.
